mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below 0.1pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving sustained equity inflows and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A consecutive 3-month decline in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained output capacity and rising input costs, compressing corporate margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or the mechanism reverses direction when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate falling below 0.50% signals extreme monetary accommodation that eliminates real yield headwinds, prompting algorithmic and institutional buying that drives positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment rates indicates structural labor market deterioration rather than cyclical softness, forecasting prolonged earnings weakness and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns in >50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, providing a fundamental earnings tailwind that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth rates produce negative 20-day forward returns in >55% of quarters, or the correlation coefficient between GDPC1 growth and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index during a supply-constrained regime signals bottleneck pressures that erode corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during supply constraints yield positive 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the signal loses significance when global commodity prices are flat.
